text: Hi guys, Luke here from Move, Train and Perform. We're going to have a look at the couch stretch,
or a modified version of the couch stretch. What we find with the rec fem stretching,
or the front of the thigh and the hip flexor stretching, is that people go too aggressive
with it. And what they tend to do is then use their back too much to compensate and offload
the stretch from the front of the thigh. So how you get into position to stretch the front
of the thigh, the rec fem hip flexor, is really really important. So this is how we want you to
go about it. So rather than jamming your leg right up against the wall, we're going to drop it down
a little bit lower. We're going to start off with the knee, the toe up on whatever this low box
you're using to elevate your foot, and then knee on a pad. Lunge out a little bit further than you
normally would with your front leg. And from here what you want to do is we're going to try and tuck
that tailbone in. We're going to try and tuck that tailbone in. We're going to try and tuck that tailbone in.
So you're going to wind your pelvis so you're closing that belt buckle area, bringing it closer to your ribs.
So you're going to wind underneath, engage this glute, and then from here once you're in this position, your
lumbar position doesn't change and your hip position doesn't change. So from there all you want to do is push back upright,
maintaining this locked lower back, tucked tailbone, squeezed glute. So you lever back with that front
foot and hold this position here. So what you're not doing is getting set and then coming up and
levering up this way. So there's lots of extension in your lower back. We're getting set, getting tight,
and then pushing back upright so that this armpit to your knee stays nice and straight. There should
be lots of tension down through the front of the hip there. And you're just going to hang out there, maintain
this activation of the back of the glute, and hold that stretch. So give it a minute to three minutes
each side. See how you go.
